changes_summary: Targeted iteration. (1) FLAG-201/correctness/callers — committed to keeping `type: 'config'` for the new-track grouped-drag branch (useClipDrag.ts:430-463) and createTrackAndMoveClip() (useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:191-217), since track creation mutates resolvedConfig.tracks[] and TimelineEditMutation.rows has no tracks field (verified at useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65). The override is constrained to the soft-tag shape (no start/children) and the type is tightened in useTimelineCommit so geometry can't leak back in. Same-track grouped drags still use `rows`. The action stays in one commit. (2) issue_hints — Step 11 now explicitly states the reorder-only case is a deliberate no-op on the timeline, with rationale and a code-comment requirement. (3) all_locations — committed to deleting pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts in Step 5 and explicitly enumerated it in Step 14. No scope growth; if anything Step 8/9 are slightly narrower because they no longer claim to remove the config branch.

success_criteria.criterion: Loading a config with legacy `pinnedShotGroups[].children` yields runtime groups in the new soft-tag shape, and saving that same timeline emits no `start` or `children` fields.

No runtime load/save/commit path re-derives clip timing or track placement from shot-group child geometry; `clips[]` remains the only source of truth for `at` and duration. This includes `rowsToConfig()`, `clonePinnedShotGroups()`, and `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry`.

`TimelineEditMutation`'s `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` field is typed as the soft-tag shape (no `start`/`children`) on both the `rows` and `config` variants, so projection geometry cannot re-enter via the override.
Dragging the right edge of the last clip in a shot changes only that clip's duration and the group's rendered right boundary follows.
Dragging the left edge of the first clip in a shot changes only that clip's start/duration and the group's rendered left boundary follows.

Dragging an interior boundary inside a shot changes only the two adjacent clips while preserving the rest of the group, and the collapsed `clip` resize session carries both clip ids and clamps without regressing the previous paired-preview behavior.

Dragging a grouped clip body translates the full group together. Same-track grouped drag commits as `type: 'rows'`. New-track grouped drag (drop onto a track that does not yet exist) commits as a single `type: 'config'` action whose `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` is the soft-tag shape and whose `nextResolvedConfig` contains the new track plus the moved clips — no split commits, no projection geometry.

Grouped cross-track moves initiated from `useTimelineTrackManagement` (drag, keyboard move-selected, create-track-and-move) all route through the soft-tag model: existing-track moves use `type: 'rows'`, new-track moves use `type: 'config'` with the soft-tag override. No calls to `moveGroup()` and no projection geometry remain.

`interactionStateRef` is created in `useTimelineState.ts` and shared with `useTimelineSave`, `useTimelinePersistence`, `usePollSync`, `useClipDrag`, and `useClipResize`; auto-save does not execute during an active drag or resize, and the newest pending save flushes after the interaction ends.

Poll-sync and conflict reloads do not overwrite local edits during an active drag or resize interaction; only the newest deferred remote payload is re-evaluated against current local state after the interaction ends.

`usePinnedShotGroups` auto-sync preserves live timing of surviving member clips: added images append at the group right edge, removed images delete only their member clip with no trailing shift, and a shot-image reorder with unchanged membership is a deliberate no-op on the timeline (documented in code).
Shot label menu actions and per-clip context-menu lockdown still work under the soft-tag model.

The video-editor test suite passes after updating intentional expectations across migrate, serialize, timeline-save-utils, render-bounds.validation, useTimelineTrackManagement, useExternalDrop, useClipEditing, useClipDrag, useShotGroups, usePollSync, and persistence tests. `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` is deleted. No new failures beyond the known unrelated pre-existing TimelineCanvas assertion if it still remains.
Net LOC or helper surface across shot-group-specific files decreases after removing projection machinery.
An unchanged timeline produces the same clip-level data for Remotion preview before and after the refactor.
Manual browser smoke testing confirms the two reported resize/save-reset symptoms no longer reproduce.

assumptions: The brief's rollback direction is correct: a structural removal of projection is simpler and safer than incremental fixes.
`(shotId, trackId)` remains the identity for a pinned group; no new stable group id is introduced.
Stored `clipIds` is ordered left-to-right by live clip `at` and is rebuilt whenever membership or position changes.

Track creation must remain a `type: 'config'` commit because `TimelineEditMutation.rows` (`useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65`) does not carry a `tracks` field. Extending `rows` with a `tracksOverride` would widen the diff for no behavioral gain; keeping `config` and tightening its `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` to the soft-tag shape is the smaller fix.
Auto-sync of pinned groups preserves live timing of surviving members; reorder-only image-list changes are an intentional no-op on the timeline.
`pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` is deleted outright (not rewritten); its role is covered by the new cohesion sequence test in Step 14 plus the per-operation tests in Steps 6–9.
Long-lived diagnostics outside the shot-group and timeline-sync paths can stay; only temporary debugging logs added during this bug cycle are removed.
